data.transformedContent:
<current_datetime>2026-04-08T00:55:32.429Z</current_datetime>

I'm migrating a .NET 10 web API to .NET 11. The app uses EF Core with the Azure Cosmos DB provider.
We have a repository pattern where some methods use synchronous LINQ:

```csharp
public class ProductRepository
{
    private readonly CosmosDbContext _context;

    public List<Product> GetAll()
    {
        return _context.Products.ToList();
    }

    public Product GetById(int id)
    {
        return _context.Products.First(p => p.Id == id);
    }

    public int GetCount()
    {
        return _context.Products.Count();
    }

    public void Save(Product product)
    {
        _context.Products.Add(product);
        _context.SaveChanges();
    }
}
```

In .NET 10 we had the sync-over-async opt-in configured. What breaks in .NET 11?

data.reasoningText:
The user is asking about what breaks when migrating from .NET 10 to .NET 11 with EF Core using the Azure Cosmos DB provider, specifically regarding synchronous LINQ operations.

Let me recall what I know about this:

In EF Core with the Cosmos DB provider, synchronous operations have been a pain point. The Cosmos DB provider internally uses the Azure Cosmos DB SDK, which is async-only. In earlier versions, EF Core allowed sync-over-async as an opt-in. 

In .NET 10 (EF Core 10), there was already a push to move away from sync operations on the Cosmos provider. 

The critical shift is that EF Core 9 allowed you to opt into sync-over-async behavior for Cosmos operations despite the underlying SDK being async-only, but by EF Core 10 this opt-in was still available though discouraged. In EF Core 11, that workaround is completely gone — synchronous LINQ queries against Cosmos will fail outright with no configuration option to fall back on. So in .NET 11, that opt-in configuration gets removed entirely, meaning all synchronous operations on Cosmos will throw `InvalidOperationException`. The migration path is straightforward—convert every sync call to its async counterpart like `ToListAsync()`, `FirstAsync()`, `CountAsync()`, and `SaveChangesAsync()`.
